---
title: "DNS issues with vpnc on Jaunty"
date: 2009-06-06
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by hikelife on 2009-06-06
I use vpnc for vpn and after I upgraded to Jaunty, it strated having DNS problem.
if I ping just the hostname on my vpn, it won't find it, if I supply .mycompanyname.com as suffix it works. resolv.conf looks good though.

before connecting through vpn:
```

~> more /etc/resolv.conf 
# Generated by NetworkManager
domain Belkin                
search Belkin                
nameserver 192.168.2.1       
nameserver 192.168.0.1       
nameserver 68.94.156.1  

```after connecting vpn
```

~> more /etc/resolv.conf                    
#@VPNC_GENERATED@ -- this file is generated by vpnc
# and will be overwritten by vpnc                  
# as long as the above mark is intact              
# Generated by NetworkManager                      
domain mycompanyname.com                                  
search Belkin                                      
nameserver 172.16.217.26
nameserver 172.16.229.26

~> ping hostxyz
ping: unknown host hostxyz

~> ping hostxyz.mycompanyname.com
PING hostxyz.mycompanyname.com (172.16.229.158) 56(84) bytes of data.
64 bytes hostxyz.mycompanyname.com (172.16.229.158) : icmp_seq=1 ttl=250 time=20.3 ms
^C

```
What's going on? Any suggestions for this?

Some config files:
```

~> vpnc --version
vpnc version 0.5.3
Built without openssl (certificate) support.
Supported DH-Groups: nopfs dh1 dh2 dh5
Supported Hash-Methods: md5 sha1
Supported Encryptions: null des 3des aes128 aes192 aes256
Supported Auth-Methods: psk psk+xauth


```

---

### Post by puppywhacker on 2009-06-06
when you add a search domain the dns resolver will try the hostxyz with all search domains until the dns server finds a positive.

```
search mycompanyname.com
```

---
